subject: Anthropology
title: Skin Color and Adaptation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Skin Color and Adaptation.

hook.text: Dark skin near the equator, light skin near the poles. It's not race — it's a map of ultraviolet radiation.

buildup.text: Skin color is an adaptation to UV intensity. Melanin protects against UV damage but blocks vitamin D production.

discovery.text: Near the equator, dark skin prevents folate destruction. At higher latitudes, lighter skin allows enough vitamin D.

twist.text: Skin color has changed multiple times in human history. It's one of the fastest-evolving and simplest adaptations.

climax.text: Skin color is biology adapting to sunlight — nothing more. It carries no information about intelligence or ability.

punchline.text: Skin color is a sunlight adaptation, not a category.

quiz.question: What determines human skin color variation?
